meta_title: For Freelancers: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to use idea validation, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship faster and build professional-grade SaaS applications.
content.answer_block: When you cannot debug code, the root cause is often an over-engineered feature set that lacks clear boundaries. By pivoting to rigorous idea validation before writing a single line of logic, you eliminate the technical debt that causes debugging nightmares, ensuring you build only what is essential for your client.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your codebase has devolved into a labyrinth of 'undefined' errors and mysterious race conditions. You are trapped in the debugging loop, patching symptoms while the actual product vision remains obscured by technical debt. This is the silent killer of freelance careers.

It doesn't have to be this way. The most successful developers don't just write code; they validate the problem space before they ever touch a keyboard. By shifting your workflow from 'coding first' to 'validating first,' you turn complex technical hurdles into predictable, modular tasks that Claude Code can handle with surgical precision.

steps.title: Define the Minimal Viable Logic
steps.description: Before debugging, simplify the feature to its core utility to ensure you aren't trying to fix a feature that shouldn't exist.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this feature request and extract the absolute minimum logic required to deliver the core value. Identify any unnecessary complexity that could lead to debugging issues.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Identify the core logic path for this module and prune all unnecessary conditional branches.'

steps.title: Automated Constraint Mapping
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map out the technical constraints of your stack so you know exactly where the code is likely to break.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dependency map and identify potential failure points in the current architecture for this feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Generate a unit test suite that targets the failure points identified in the architecture map.'

steps.title: Refactoring for Readability
steps.description: If you can't debug it, your code is likely too dense. Use Claude Code to force modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function into smaller, single-responsibility modules to improve testability and error tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Decompose the main.ts logic into isolated, testable utility functions.'

steps.title: Validation-Driven Implementation
steps.description: Implement the feature using a test-first approach, ensuring that every line of code serves a validated requirement.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the implementation for this feature, ensuring that every block of code has a corresponding validation check.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Write the code following the TDD approach for the validated modules.'

steps.title: Final Debugging Cycle
steps.description: With a validated, modular codebase, use Claude Code to perform a final sweep for edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the final implementation and suggest edge cases that might still cause runtime errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Run the integrated test suite and fix all remaining edge-case errors.'

pro_tips: Never debug a monolith; always extract the problematic logic into a standalone script before asking for a fix.
Use Claude Code to document your 'why' in the comments, not just the 'what', to prevent future debugging sessions.
If you find yourself stuck for more than 15 minutes, delete the function and regenerate it from scratch using a clearer prompt.
Leverage Antigravity to simulate user input patterns; often, bugs are just unexpected data shapes.
Treat your prompt history as a version control system—save successful architectural prompts for future client projects.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, begin building a repository of your 'validated' prompt templates to speed up your next project by 50%.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start building the future. By mastering idea validation and AI-assisted workflows, you reclaim your time and scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
